name: Cinnamon Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Place water, dry milk, and yeast in a mixer bowl; mix lightly and let set for 5 minutes.
2. Add 1 1/2 cups flour to the liquid mixture. Mix at medium speed using a dough hook for 4 minutes.
3. Add ginger, oil, salt, cinnamon, sweetener, and 1 1/2 cups flour to the batter. Mix at low speed using a dough hook for another 4 minutes. Use as much of the remaining flour as necessary to make a smooth resilient dough.
4. Shape the dough into a ball and place in a bowl well greased with margarine. Turn the ball over to coat the top with margarine. Cover with a cloth and set in a warm place until doubled in volume.
5. Turn dough onto a lightly floured working surface and knead lightly. Form into a ball and return to the greased bowl, turning the top again to cover it with margarine. Cover with a cloth and set in a warm place until doubled in volume.
6. Preheat oven to 375°F. Use 1/2 teaspoon margarine to grease a 9 by 13 inch cake pan. Set aside for later use.
7. Turn dough onto a lightly floured working surface. Knead lightly and form into a ball. Cover with a cloth and let rest 10 minutes.
8. Roll dough out to form a 9 by 16-inch rectangle. Spread the softened 1/2 teaspoon margarine evenly over the dough.
9. Sprinkle evenly with the brown sugar substitute and cinnamon mixture.
10. Roll into a long roll like a jelly roll and cut into 24 equal slices. Place the slices, cut side down, in the cake pan, spacing them evenly.
11. Cover with a cloth and let rise until doubled in volume.
12. Bake at 375°F for 25 to 30 minutes or until golden brown. Turn rolls out of the pan onto a wire rack and serve warm, if possible.
Energy per 1 roll serving: 99 calories; CHO 14 gm; PRO 2 gm; FAT 4 gm; NA 107 mg
Low-sodium diets: Omit salt. Use salt-free margarine.
